briefdescription.para: This function is the main entry point for the HDCP example using the XDpRxSs driver.

detaileddescription.para: This function will enable HDCP during DisplayPort RX Subsystem set up to work in MST/SST mode.

parameterdescription.para: is the unique device ID of the DisplayPort RX Subsystem core.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if DisplayPort RX Subsystem HDCP enabled successfully.

briefdescription.para: This function initialize required platform specific peripherals.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if required peripherals are initialized and configured successfully.
XST_SUCCESS if Video Phy configured successfully.

briefdescription.para: This function configures DisplayPort RX Subsystem.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if DPRX Subsystem configured successfully.

briefdescription.para: This is the main function for XDpRxSs SST/MST example.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the MST/SST example passed.
XST_FAILURE if the MST/SST example was unsuccessful.

briefdescription.para: This file contains a design example using the XDpRxSs driver in single stream (SST) transport or multi-stream transport (MST) mode and enables HDCP.

simplesect.para: For this example to display output, the user need to implement initialization of the system (DpRxSs_PlatformInit), Video Phy (DpRxSs_VideoPhyInit), start DisplayPort RX subsystem (XDpRxSs_Start) and DisplayPort RX Subsystem setup (DpRxSs_Setup). The input to the Subsystem is from RX (GT). The functions DpRxSs_PlatformInit, DpRxSs_VideoPhyInit and DpRxSs_Setup are declared and are left up to the user to implement. In addition to the setups, this example requires to implement required interrupts to view the output.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.00 sha 10/05/15 Initial release. 4.00 ms 01/23/17 Modified xil_printf statement in main function to ensure that "Successfully ran" and "Failed" strings are available in all examples. This is a fix for CR-965028.
